audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Isaac's dim eyes, Rebekah/Jacob deception, blessing language, Jacob/Esau wordplay, trembling, stolen blessing accusation, and Esau's hatred.

verses.restored: When Isaac was old and his eyes were dim from seeing, he called Esau his older son and said to him, My son. He said to him, Here I am.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, that when Isaac was old, and his eyes were dim...

notes.title: Dim eyes
notes.body: Isaac’s impaired sight sets the stage for misrecognition and deception.

verses.restored: He said, Look, I am old; I do not know the day of my death.

familiar.text: And he said, Behold now, I am old, I know not the day of my death:

verses.restored: Now take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, go out to the field, and hunt game for me.

familiar.text: Now therefore take, I pray thee, thy weapons, thy quiver and thy bow...

verses.restored: Make me tasty food, such as I love, and bring it to me so I may eat, that my soul may bless you before I die.

familiar.text: And make me savoury meat, such as I love...

verses.restored: Rebekah was listening when Isaac spoke to Esau his son. Esau went to the field to hunt game and bring it.

familiar.text: And Rebekah heard when Isaac spake to Esau his son...

verses.restored: Rebekah spoke to Jacob her son, saying, Look, I heard your father speaking to Esau your brother, saying,

familiar.text: And Rebekah spake unto Jacob her son, saying...

verses.restored: Bring me game and make me tasty food, so I may eat and bless you before the Eternal before my death.

familiar.text: Bring me venison, and make me savoury meat, that I may eat...

verses.restored: Now, my son, listen to my voice, to what I command you.

familiar.text: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice according to that which I command thee.

notes.title: Listen to my voice
notes.body: Rebekah’s instruction echoes the recurring theme of whose voice is heard and obeyed.

verses.restored: Go now to the flock and take for me from there two good young goats, and I will make them tasty food for your father, such as he loves.

familiar.text: Go now to the flock, and fetch me from thence two good kids of the goats...

verses.restored: You shall bring it to your father, and he shall eat, so that he may bless you before his death.

familiar.text: And thou shalt bring it to thy father, that he may eat...

verses.restored: Jacob said to Rebekah his mother, Look, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man.

familiar.text: And Jacob said to Rebekah his mother, Behold, Esau my brother is a hairy man...

verses.restored: Perhaps my father will feel me, and I shall be in his eyes like a deceiver, and I shall bring upon myself a curse and not a blessing.

familiar.text: My father peradventure will feel me, and I shall seem to him as a deceiver...

notes.title: Like a deceiver
notes.body: Jacob fears exposure and curse more than the moral problem of deception itself.

verses.restored: His mother said to him, Your curse be on me, my son. Only listen to my voice, and go, take them for me.

familiar.text: And his mother said unto him, Upon me be thy curse, my son...

verses.restored: He went and took them and brought them to his mother, and his mother made tasty food such as his father loved.

familiar.text: And he went, and fetched, and brought them to his mother...

verses.restored: Rebekah took the desirable garments of Esau her older son, which were with her in the house, and clothed Jacob her younger son.

familiar.text: And Rebekah took goodly raiment of her eldest son Esau...

verses.restored: She put the skins of the young goats on his hands and on the smooth part of his neck.

familiar.text: And she put the skins of the kids of the goats upon his hands...

verses.restored: She gave the tasty food and the bread that she had made into the hand of Jacob her son.

familiar.text: And she gave the savoury meat and the bread...

verses.restored: He came to his father and said, My father. He said, Here I am. Who are you, my son?

familiar.text: And he came unto his father, and said, My father: and he said, Here am I...

verses.restored: Jacob said to his father, I am Esau your firstborn. I have done as you spoke to me. Rise now, sit, and eat from my game, so that your soul may bless me.

familiar.text: And Jacob said unto his father, I am Esau thy firstborn...

notes.title: False identity claim
notes.body: Jacob directly claims Esau’s identity and firstborn status.

verses.restored: Isaac said to his son, How is it that you found it so quickly, my son? He said, Because the Eternal your Divine made it happen before me.

familiar.text: And Isaac said unto his son, How is it that thou hast found it so quickly...

notes.title: Divine name used in deception
notes.body: Jacob invokes the Eternal inside the deception, heightening the moral tension.

verses.restored: Isaac said to Jacob, Please come near, so I may feel you, my son, whether you are truly my son Esau or not.

familiar.text: And Isaac said unto Jacob, Come near, I pray thee, that I may feel thee...

verses.restored: Jacob came near to Isaac his father, and he felt him and said, The voice is Jacob’s voice, but the hands are Esau’s hands.

familiar.text: And Jacob went near unto Isaac his father; and he felt him...

notes.title: Voice and hands
notes.body: Isaac perceives contradiction between sound and touch.

verses.restored: He did not recognize him, because his hands were hairy like Esau his brother’s hands, and he blessed him.

familiar.text: And he discerned him not, because his hands were hairy...

verses.restored: He said, Are you truly my son Esau? He said, I am.

familiar.text: And he said, Art thou my very son Esau? And he said, I am.

verses.restored: He said, Bring it near to me, and I will eat from my son’s game, so that my soul may bless you. He brought it near to him, and he ate; he brought him wine, and he drank.

familiar.text: And he said, Bring it near to me, and I will eat...

verses.restored: Isaac his father said to him, Please come near and kiss me, my son.

familiar.text: And his father Isaac said unto him, Come near now, and kiss me...

verses.restored: He came near and kissed him. He smelled the smell of his garments and blessed him, saying, See, the smell of my son is like the smell of a field that the Eternal has blessed.

familiar.text: And he came near, and kissed him: and he smelled the smell of his raiment...

verses.restored: May the Divine give you from the dew of the heavens and from the fatness of the earth, and abundance of grain and new wine.

familiar.text: Therefore God give thee of the dew of heaven, and the fatness of the earth...

verses.restored: Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be master over your brothers, and let your mother’s sons bow down to you. Cursed be those who curse you, and blessed be those who bless you.

familiar.text: Let people serve thee, and nations bow down to thee...

notes.title: Reversal blessing
notes.body: This blessing echoes the prenatal oracle: the older shall serve the younger.

verses.restored: As soon as Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, and Jacob had just gone out from the face of Isaac his father, Esau his brother came in from his hunting.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, as soon as Isaac had made an end of blessing Jacob...

verses.restored: He also made tasty food and brought it to his father. He said to his father, Let my father rise and eat from his son’s game, so that your soul may bless me.

familiar.text: And he also had made savoury meat, and brought it unto his father...

verses.restored: Isaac his father said to him, Who are you? He said, I am your son, your firstborn, Esau.

familiar.text: And Isaac his father said unto him, Who art thou? And he said, I am thy son...

verses.restored: Isaac trembled with a very great trembling and said, Who then was he who hunted game and brought it to me? I ate from all before you came, and I blessed him. Yes, blessed he shall be.

familiar.text: And Isaac trembled very exceedingly, and said, Who? where is he...

notes.title: Very great trembling
notes.body: The Hebrew intensifies Isaac’s shock. Yet he does not revoke the blessing.

verses.restored: When Esau heard his father’s words, he cried out with a great and very bitter cry and said to his father, Bless me, me also, my father!

familiar.text: And when Esau heard the words of his father, he cried with a great and exceeding bitter cry...

verses.restored: He said, Your brother came with deceit and took your blessing.

familiar.text: And he said, Thy brother came with subtilty, and hath taken away thy blessing.

verses.restored: He said, Is he not rightly named Jacob? For he has taken my place these two times: he took my birthright, and look, now he has taken my blessing. He said, Have you not reserved a blessing for me?

familiar.text: And he said, Is not he rightly named Jacob? for he hath supplanted me...

notes.title: Jacob wordplay
notes.body: Esau links Jacob’s name with taking the heel/supplanting.

verses.restored: Isaac answered and said to Esau, Look, I have made him master over you, and all his brothers I have given to him as servants. With grain and new wine I have sustained him. What then can I do for you, my son?

familiar.text: And Isaac answered and said unto Esau, Behold, I have made him thy lord...

verses.restored: Esau said to his father, Have you only one blessing, my father? Bless me, me also, my father. And Esau lifted his voice and wept.

familiar.text: And Esau said unto his father, Hast thou but one blessing, my father?...

verses.restored: Isaac his father answered and said to him, Look, away from the fatness of the earth shall your dwelling be, and away from the dew of the heavens above.

familiar.text: And Isaac his father answered and said unto him, Behold, thy dwelling shall be the fatness of the earth...

notes.title: Away from / of
notes.body: The Hebrew preposition can be read as from/of. Context contrasts Esau’s blessing with Jacob’s.

verses.restored: By your sword you shall live, and your brother you shall serve. But when you grow restless, you shall break his yoke from your neck.

familiar.text: And by thy sword shalt thou live, and shalt serve thy brother...

verses.restored: Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing with which his father had blessed him. Esau said in his heart, The days of mourning for my father are near; then I will kill Jacob my brother.

familiar.text: And Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing wherewith his father blessed him...

verses.restored: The words of Esau her older son were told to Rebekah. She sent and called Jacob her younger son and said to him, Look, Esau your brother is comforting himself concerning you by planning to kill you.

familiar.text: And these words of Esau her elder son were told to Rebekah...

verses.restored: Now, my son, listen to my voice. Rise, flee to Laban my brother in Haran.

familiar.text: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice; and arise, flee thou to Laban...

verses.restored: Stay with him a few days, until your brother’s heat turns away,

familiar.text: And tarry with him a few days, until thy brother's fury turn away;

verses.restored: until your brother’s anger turns away from you and he forgets what you have done to him. Then I will send and take you from there. Why should I be bereaved of both of you in one day?

familiar.text: Until thy brother's anger turn away from thee, and he forget that which thou hast done...

verses.restored: Rebekah said to Isaac, I am disgusted with my life because of the daughters of Heth. If Jacob takes a woman from the daughters of Heth like these, from the daughters of the land, why should I live?

familiar.text: And Rebekah said to Isaac, I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth...

notes.title: Marriage pretext
notes.body: Rebekah frames Jacob’s departure around marriage, while the immediate danger is Esau’s anger.
